mechanism_narrative: A high unemployment rate indicates a weak labor market that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months when UNRATE exceeds 6%, or rank IC of UNRATE level vs forward returns is not negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A level of industrial production below the 2017 base (100) signals contraction in the manufacturing sector, which historically correlates with economic weakness and negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO below 100 is associated with positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances, or rank IC is not reliably negative over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 800 thousand units signals a downturn in residential investment, which historically precedes broader economic weakening and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 800, 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or the mean return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.5% indicates severe labor market slack that reduces consumer demand and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE > 6.5%, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When growth in retail sales (demand) outpaces growth in industrial production (supply), inventories are drawn down, leading to future production increases and positive equity returns over a quarter. The interaction of both series provides a clearer signal than either alone.
failure_condition: If the spread between RSAFS year-over-year growth and INDPRO year-over-year growth does not show a consistent positive correlation with 60-day forward returns (rank IC < 0.10 over 5 years).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a very low federal funds rate and a falling unemployment rate creates an accommodative policy environment in a strengthening economy, which is highly supportive of equity markets and yields positive forward returns.
regime_hint: accommodative policy with improving labor market
failure_condition: If in periods when FEDFUNDS < 1% and UNRATE is declining, 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market (unemployment below 5%), rising payroll employment no longer signals economic strength but instead fuels wage inflation that squeezes margins and prompts Fed tightening, causing negative equity returns.
regime_hint: tight labor market with UNRATE below 5%
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 5%, rising PAYEMS is associated with positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances, contradicting the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (federal funds rate below 2%), rising inflation erodes real bond returns and creates expectations of future rate hikes, which negatively impact equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 2%, periods of rising CPI are not followed by negative 60-day returns (rank IC < 0.05 or mean return non-negative).
